title: Rhinanthus × brigantiacus
taxonomy: Rhinanthus × brigantiacus is a hybrid species within the plant family Orobanchaceae. It belongs to the genus Rhinanthus, which consists of parasitic flowering plants. The hybrid is known to be a cross between Rhinanthus angustifolius and Rhinanthus sardous. Rhinanthus × brigantiacus is primarily found in certain regions of Europe. This hybrid species is often recognized by its unique characteristics and flowering patterns.

intro: Rhinanthus × brigantiacus, also known as the Yellow Rampion, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the broomrape family (Orobanchaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Rhinanthus × brigantiacus include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, such as aucubin and rosmarinic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into capsules and tablets, allowing for its potential health benefits to be harnessed in various forms.
